candidate_problems.text: Identify a pair of comparable cities where one achieved durable safety gains and test whether differences in legitimacy, clearance, public-space governance, and adaptive learning jointly explain divergence better than single-factor accounts.
Determine whether there are multiple distinct hard-to-vary safety equilibria, or one common constraint network underlying most long-run crime declines.

Test whether cities can sustain long-run safety improvements when one proposed central element—such as legitimacy or case clearance—is weak, indicating the conjectured network may be less load bearing than claimed.
